extraction.one_line_summary: DEMARE Arnold won a chaotic sprint in Vittel after CAVENDISH crashed at 1.3km attempting to squeeze past SAGAN, then crashed again in final meters.
tactical_patterns: Dimension Data's leadout with Renshaw collapsed early when Cavendish wasn't positioned, forcing him into desperate gap-closing maneuvers.
Sagan contested the sprint despite team role usually being stage hunting, revealing Quick-Step didn't control positioning into final kilometer.
Demare isolated from teammates still won, suggesting French champion was strongest pure sprinter regardless of leadout quality.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: twice crashed in final 1.3km trying to force gaps rather than patient positioning, suggests desperation or poor form
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: challenged for sprint win despite domestique expectations, but positioning contributed to Cavendish's first crash
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: had Greipel's wheel and team support but faded to third, lacked finishing punch
rider_signals.GREIPEL Andre: negative: positioned second wheel behind Renshaw but completely faded when sprint opened
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnold: positive: won isolated without proper leadout, pure power overcame positioning disadvantage
